items.title: LIVE: Nationwide ‘No Kings Day’ protests counter Trump parade | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>WARNING: MAY CONTAIN STRONG LANGUAGE OR GRAPHIC IMAGES

Anti-Trump activists plan nationwide protests, as a military parade and festival in Washington D.C. marks the 250th anniversary of the US Army. The day is also US President Donald Trump's 79th birthday.

Follow our live coverage: https://reut.rs/3HBJ8yh

#protest #NoKingsDay #Trump #DonaldTrump #protests #parade #militaryparade #live #Reuters #news

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>

items.title: LIVE: Trump holds military parade on his birthday for US Army's 250th anniversary
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>A military parade, festival and fireworks on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., mark the U.S. Army's 250th birthday. The event coincides with U.S. President Trump’s 79th birthday and includes a wreath-laying ceremony, fitness competition, equipment displays and military demonstrations.

#Live #parade #MilitaryParade #USArmy #WashingtonDC #Trump

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>

items.title: Thousands attend anti-Trump protests nationwide | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Hundreds of thousands of Americans attended rallies across the country to protest President Donald Trump's aggressive approach in major cities.

items.title: Marines carry out first known detention of civilian in Los Angeles | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Marines deployed to Los Angeles temporarily detained a civilian on June 13, the U.S. military confirmed after being presented with Reuters images, in the first known detention by active-duty troops deployed there by President Donald Trump.

#detention #LosAngeles #Marines #protest  #LA #Marine #troops #News #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: 'Anything goes': artist Yoshitomo Nara as London exhibition opens | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Artworks by Yoshitomo Nara go on display in London this week in what organizers say is the largest European retrospective of the Japanese artist. ‘This is about 40 years worth of my work,’ Nara told Reuters at a press preview.

items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>US Marines deployed to Los Angeles made their first detention of a civilian, part of a rare domestic use of its forces sent to the city after days of protests over immigration raids.
